HGSS.698.0001:
Hey, how about we exchange phone
numbers before we eat?[0200,0000]
HGSS.698.0002:
I’ve always got my Pokégear in reach,
so if you give me a call, I’ll answer\r
pretty quick!
HGSS.698.0003:
Don’t worry!\f
I’m a really nice Camper...
HGSS.698.0004:
Did you decide you’d like to trade
numbers with me after all?[0200,0000]
HGSS.698.0005:
Yeah! Hey, here you are!\f
I’ve been holding on to this just for
you. Don’t be shy--go ahead and\r
take it. It’s a present!\f
HGSS.698.0006:
Hm? Oh, you’re just around the corner?\f
If you’re that close, why bother with
a phone call? Humph!
HGSS.698.0007:
You know, it’s perfectly fine to be all
affectionate to your Pokémon and let\r
them live in your house.\f
But I prefer to go out camping with my
constant companion, [0100,000A,0000],\r
which is why it’s so strong!\f
That reminds me--we knocked out a wild
[0100,000B,0000], whammo! Just like that,\r
the other day. You should’ve seen it...
HGSS.698.0008:
About all this training your Pokémon
just so you can catch stronger\r
wild Pokémon...\f
It’s not a good idea to be throwing
Poké Balls around recklessly, trying to\r
catch any old thing.\f
You need to build up a close connection
with your Pokémon.\f
My relationship with my [0100,000A,0000] is
a perfect example.\f
...Well, I say that, but the other day
we had a wild [0100,000B,0000] just barely\r
get away from us.\f
No matter how much I’d rather not think
about it, I can’t let the thoughts go...
HGSS.698.0009:
Hear me out! Earlier, we met a wild
[0100,000B,0000]!\f
It kind of jumped out as we were on the
way home from a camping excursion!\r
I was so surprised!\f
I guess it goes to show you shouldn’t
let your guard down when you’re out\r
in nature!
HGSS.698.0010:
I was getting ready to cook up some
chow, and I started thinking about you.\f
Before I sit down to eat, would you like
to battle again?\f
I’m on [0104,0003,0000], so bring your
best Pokémon!
HGSS.698.0011:
Huh? What’s going on? Did you get lost
or something?\f
Stay calm... I am right smack on
[0104,0003,0000], so you should\r
have no trouble finding me!
HGSS.698.0012:
Do you know the difference between
going on a picnic and camping?\f
It’s a song, of course!\f
When you’re going on a picnic, you sing a
happy, upbeat little tune as you hike\r
over hills and fields.\f
When you’re camping, you sing quiet,
mournful songs of grief and loss while\r
sitting and staring at the campfire.\f
What? You don’t believe me?\f
Then we should go camping sometime
and sit around the fire and see what\r
kind of melodies you feel like singing!
HGSS.698.0013:
Recently, I was digging a fire pit in the
ground so I could cook supper, and\r
I found an unusual item!\f
Apparently this thing is called a
Sun Stone.\f
I’ll give it to you. I’m waiting on
[0104,0003,0000]!
HGSS.698.0014:
I still have that Sun Stone I said I’d
give you!\f
I’m waiting on [0104,0003,0000], so
come and get it as soon as you can!
